characters.description: The founder of the mobile-city project, working with Wendy to forge a mechanical replacement for his missing left arm. His command of rare materials lets him undertake construction that other engineers cannot safely perform.
characters.name: Randidly Ghosthound

characters.description: The chief engineer behind Randidly's mechanical city. She directs its design work but depends on him to shape materials beyond her level and technical reach.
characters.name: Wendy

characters.description: An engraving specialist who assists Wendy and the apprentices constructing Randidly's moving city.
characters.name: Lucretia

characters.description: A spear fighter who joins the expedition investigating reports of a dragon on Mount Olympus.
characters.name: Alana

characters.description: The archer in the Mount Olympus expedition investigating a reported dragon.
characters.name: Annie

characters.description: The leader of Zone 11 and a member of the Mount Olympus expedition investigating a reported dragon.
characters.name: Warlord

characters.description: A level-79 dragon that emerges from Mount Olympus after Alana kills one of its young. Its freezing attack overwhelms Alana while other dragons rise around them.
characters.name: Ice Dragon Broodmother

characters.description: The administrator of Randidly's company. She recognizes that a government land proposal is designed to gain leverage over the organization and handles the negotiation with caution.
characters.name: Tatiana

characters.description: A formidable ally of Randidly who is available to defend the company from the monsters gathering nearby, though she longs for more demanding opponents.
characters.name: Helen

characters.description: A politically connected senator whose aide advances a coercive proposal for the government to acquire the company's land and lease it back.
characters.name: Senator Heathridge

characters.description: An operative aligned with Senator Heathridge who tries to use a rival engineering order and a nearby monster threat to pressure Randidly's company.
characters.name: Evan Crane

characters.description: The leader of Randidly's training order. Having endured Randidly's demanding personal instruction, he now oversees an equally severe program for new aspirants.
characters.name: Naffer

characters.description: An aspirant in Randidly's training order who persists through raids, weighted movement, and repeated duels. Her determination is tested by the program's escalating demands.
characters.name: Vi

characters.description: A training-order applicant who works beside Vi during the order's demanding program.
characters.name: Garrett

characters.description: A woman who arranged Randidly's earlier dungeon encounter and bargains to provide information about the System in exchange for a class.
characters.name: The woman seeking a class

characters.description: The Order Dukies aspirant previously introduced as Vi, now identified by her full name. She formerly worked around Randidly's company as an assistant to Tatiana.
characters.name: Vi Riverson

characters.description: The Order Dukies applicant previously introduced as Garrett, now identified by his full name. He assists Vi during the company festival.
characters.name: Garrett Tysven

characters.description: Randidly's developing fire image, which begins changing while he forges and engraves a difficult promised vessel.
characters.name: Ignition Essence

characters.description: A composite biological intelligence formed from six brains and able to work through mechanical drones. It warns Randidly of several emerging dangers and requests an open future favor in return for helping his city project.
characters.name: Ghost

characters.description: Randidly's father, reported to have left a trail of deaths after separating from Hank Howard. His purpose and present destination are unclear.
characters.name: Ezekiel Ghosthound

characters.description: An unidentified killer moving south whose dead victims have been drained of blood and whose survivors are left mentally broken.
characters.name: Stroud

characters.description: A specialist who arrives with a companion to address the damage to Randidly's class.
characters.name: Nathan

characters.description: Randidly's world-tree image and one of the animi used in Nathan's effort to stabilize his damaged class.
characters.name: Yggdrasil

characters.description: A village spirit who is also a human individual seeking greater autonomy. She brings Randidly information about Shawl's failed Nexus test and the accelerated calamity facing his world, while trying to regain Randidly's trust.
characters.name: Lyra

characters.description: The financially strained Zone 1 power broker previously known as Senator Heathridge. He seeks control of Randidly's company and equips Evan Crane with an experimental exosuit.
characters.name: Senator Cliff Heathridge

characters.description: A companion bound to Randidly who is investigating the dangerous toad world and developing greater control over raw ether. She confronts and comforts him before they spar ahead of his departure.
characters.name: Nevaeh

characters.description: A hostile investigator who enters Karen’s reactor with Evan Crane and Senator Cliff Heathridge. The group accuses Randidly of using imports that violate Zone 7 law.
characters.name: Chuck Ballard

characters.description: The developing spirit of Randidly's mobile city, able to decide independently when and where the city moves.
characters.name: Karen

characters.description: A dragon-headed liaison who introduces Randidly and his separated images to the Nether warfront, explains its hazards, and links his own advancement to their performance.
characters.name: Zagnall

characters.description: The manifested form of Randidly's fire image, previously known as Ignition Essence. It arrives at the Nether warfront alongside the Grim Chimera and Yggdrasil.
characters.name: Ignition of the Emerald Essence

characters.description: A principal image of Randidly that awakens with his active consciousness at the Nether warfront while his physical body remains confined.
characters.name: Grim Chimera

characters.description: The local lord who directs newly arrived images at the Nether warfront and assigns them to fight under designated liaisons.
characters.name: Lord Milne

characters.description: A powerful eastern front-line leader to whose command Randidly is assigned after his first mission.
characters.name: The feathered commander

characters.description: The feathered commander's lieutenant, who escorts Randidly from her tent to the staging area for his Great Rift mission.
characters.name: The granite-bodied lieutenant

characters.description: A gauntleted front-line fighter with turquoise hair who briefly approaches Randidly during his training, then withdraws with her two companions before explaining her purpose.
characters.name: The turquoise-haired soldier

characters.description: A silent, rock-bodied woman assigned to serve under Randidly at the front. He suspects she may also be observing him for someone else.
characters.name: The rock-bodied subordinate

characters.description: An anxious, snake-headed fighter assigned to Randidly's front-line group. He carries two small scimitars.
characters.name: The snake-headed subordinate

characters.description: The warfront authority who retains custody of Randidly’s imprisoned physical body while his conscious image serves in the fighting.
characters.name: The supreme commander

characters.description: An unnamed old woman who shares control of the fate armory and possesses image mastery beyond the nearby commanders.
characters.name: The fate-armory elder

characters.description: An elder who guards the fate armory and leads Randidly to the dormant sword blade once carried by her dead daughter. She warns that accepting it could consume him.
characters.name: Nadia

characters.description: A member of Randidly's strategic-key party who remains with the group after its dangerous visit to the fate armory.
characters.name: Salazar

characters.description: A borderlands refugee recently brought to Karen and Tatiana's former partner from before the System. She has an affinity with moss spirits and openly questions Randidly's leadership and absence.
characters.name: Felicia

characters.description: Ezekiel's helmeted combat companion, who fights under the persona Dauntless. He uses a sea-and-ship image against the blood beasts surrounding a healer's cabin.
characters.name: Ace

characters.description: Zone 19’s leading healer. She resists an abduction attempt by Ezekiel and Ace and can use holy power to eradicate blood beasts, although repeated use leaves her exhausted.
characters.name: The red-haired healer

characters.description: A maker who completes a seven-dawn forging ritual to produce a massive shield for Earth's defense against a possible Ogre invasion.
characters.name: Sam

characters.description: The first chairwoman elected by the World Summit. Her one-year term places her at the head of the emerging coalition among Earth's zones.
characters.name: Theodora Grayman

characters.description: A commander who has recaptured the escaped Nether Prince.
characters.name: Commander Tarith
